idea.hook: How long to reach Andromeda, our galactic neighbor?
facts: At light speed, the journey would take 2.537 million years.
Our fastest probe (Parker Solar Probe at ~200 km/s) would take over 3.8 BILLION years.
idea.payoff: That's nearly the entire age of Earth in travel time!
idea.title: Andromeda: A 3.8 BILLION Year Trip?! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: How long to reach Andromeda, our galactic neighbor?
scenes.visual: A captivating, slowly rotating visual of the Milky Way and Andromeda galaxies, subtly approaching each other in a vast cosmic void.
A deep space background with stars. Text appears, then a graphic of the two galaxies appears, with a numerical distance '2.537M LY' visually highlighted between them.
The space background from Scene 2. A vibrant beam of light streaks across the screen from left to right, representing light speed. The '2.537 MILLION YEARS' text highlights brightly.

scenes.text: Our fastest probe (~200 km/s)? Over 3.8 BILLION YEARS.
scenes.visual: A detailed 3D render of a futuristic space probe (like the Parker Solar Probe) slowly traversing a starry field, emphasizing its slowness compared to the vastness. The '3.8 BILLION YEARS' text flashes on screen.

scenes.text: That's nearly the ENTIRE AGE OF EARTH in travel time!
scenes.visual: A visual timeline or infographic comparing the '3.8 BILLION years' to 'Earth's age (4.5 Billion years)'. Perhaps a rotating Earth image alongside the monumental travel time number for emphasis.
